decisive_pair_note: Layer-1 shape and both branch cards (curl_129_h1, curl_165_h1) name the decisive pair as fast>minimizer (aflfast_rarity). minimizer = PowerSchedule None (calibration-only energy); fast = AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rare-edge power schedule. Substitute naive for minimizer only if the campaign lacks a minimizer arm and naive is the confirmed flat-energy baseline.

hypotheses.label: fast over-invests havoc energy in the rare-edge seed, so its corpus shows more exploration past the rare edge than minimizer's

measurement.registry_tool_note: No existing registry tool measures scheduling energy allocation. operand_enrichment is for fixed-offset VALUE gates (this is a presence/non-null gate, no literal); joint_necessity is for vpc assembly-depth; value_distance_reached needs operand_literal; depth_reach re-reads the resolve pattern (see decidable_rationale). NEW descriptor proposed; orchestrator builds it ONLY if a per-seed energy ledger ever ships.

params.rare_edge_seed: the lowest-n_fuzz seed in fast's corpus that hits the gate's caller edge (Curl_http_host @ http.c:2157 for curl_129; Curl_http_cookies @ http.c:2792 for curl_165) but NOT the target branch
params.descendant_share: fraction of corpus seeds that are lineage-descendants of the rare-edge seed (or, if lineage parentage is unavailable, fraction whose covered-edge set is a strict superset of the rare-edge seed's) — computed identically for fast and minimizer corpora, expressed as fast / minimizer ratio
params.depth_reach: max coverage-edge depth past the rare edge reached by any descendant, fast minus minimizer

alternative_descriptor.use_when: seed_lineage parentage is missing for the fast/minimizer corpora; fall back to the per-trial coverage-timeseries growth rate of edges in the rare-edge region (functions reachable only past the gate's caller) as fast-vs-minimizer slope ratio

hypotheses.prediction: G1: If the win is the rare-edge POWER SCHEDULE, fast must spend disproportionate budget mutating the rare-edge seed, so fast's corpus shows MORE descendants of / deeper reach past the rare edge than minimizer's (rare_seed_descendant_share fast/minimizer >= 1.5 AND rare_seed_depth_reach > 0). A rival 'lucky seed / no scheduling effect' mechanism predicts fast and minimizer explore the rare edge equally (ratio ~ 1, depth diff ~ 0) — the win would then be variance, not the schedule. The measurement is INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern (G2): it scores corpus exploration AROUND the rare edge, not whether fast resolved the target branch.
hypotheses.decidable_rationale: G3 HONESTY: aflfast's effect is a SCHEDULING dynamic — WHICH seed gets energy WHEN, accumulated over the campaign — not a corpus-content fingerprint. fast and minimizer share a byte-identical havoc mutator vocabulary (per data_realities, mutation_op cannot distinguish them) and SAVE THE SAME KINDS OF BYTES; they only budget them differently. So NO terminal-corpus tool discriminates them: the only trace of the energy boost is corpus SCALE/SHAPE, which is confounded by run-to-run variance, total exec budget, and seed-admission luck. With n=2 branches on ONE target (curl) and no per-seed energy ledger in seed_lineage, 'more descendants past the rare edge' cannot be cleanly attributed to the power schedule vs. ordinary stochastic drift. A per-seed ENERGY LEDGER (exec count attributed per seed over time) would make this decidable, but the campaign does not ship one, and a seed_lineage scheduling-event descriptor is FORBIDDEN by data_realities (same class as the 'I2S event'). Mark decidable:false and let fallback carry these branches to inconclusive.

data_realities_respected: Not a VALUE gate (presence_or_nonnull, operand_literal null) -> operand_enrichment (byte_freq_ratio) returns no_gate_signature; inapplicable, not proposed.
Not a vpc joint-assembly gate, no vpc arm -> joint_necessity (struct_size_and_token_count) inapplicable; not proposed.
operand_literal is null -> value_distance_reached has no target operand to compute Hamming distance against; inapplicable, not proposed.

depth_reach is CIRCULAR here (G2): it reads how deep the WINNER-resolving seed set drives the branch, but for a scheduling mechanism the winner's seeds ARE the seeds fast evolved via the very energy boost under test — a deeper reading just re-confirms fast resolved it and cannot separate the schedule from a lucky seed. Not proposed.
mutation_op is byte-identical / untagged across fuzzers -> NO seed_lineage:event_cooccurrence 'scheduling event' descriptor (infeasible, same class as the forbidden 'I2S event').

Both branches are curl (on-disk corpus on s4), so a corpus/lineage descriptor is at least populatable, but n=2 single-target is too thin to power any rule and the signal itself is variance-confounded.
notes: ROUND-1 RE-DESIGN VERDICT: WHOLE-SHAPE decidable:false -> both UNLABELED branches (curl_129, curl_165) route to inconclusive honestly (G3). REASONING: aflfast-rarity is a SCHEDULING (non-local) technique whose advantage is a campaign dynamic — the AFLFast FAST n_fuzz rare-edge power schedule gives rarely-hit coverage buckets up to ~4x energy vs minimizer's PowerSchedule None calibration-only energy — and it leaves NO per-branch content fingerprint, since fast and minimizer save the same byte kinds and differ only in WHICH seed gets budget WHEN. PRIORS KEPT: rare_edge_energy_overdrive (the only prior hypothesis) is retained verbatim and remains correctly decidable:false; the brief forbids a relaxed-threshold re-test of it. NEW MECHANISMS CONSIDERED + REJECTED: I reviewed every built tool not yet tried for a concrete discriminating signal and found NONE applicable: operand_enrichment / value_distance_reached both require an operand literal these presence gates lack; joint_necessity requires a vpc assembly-depth gate; depth_reach is G2-circular (its winner-seed depth reading is selected by the very resolve pattern that defines the shape, so it cannot tell the schedule from a lucky seed). corpus_size_ratio / token_count would just measure corpus scale, which is variance/exec-budget confounded and not attributable to the schedule per-branch. Inventing such a ratio to force a label would violate G3. WHAT WOULD MAKE IT DECIDABLE: a per-seed energy/exec ledger attributed over campaign time (seed_lineage:energy_share) — not present, and a lineage scheduling-event descriptor is explicitly forbidden by data_realities. COLLAPSE: the two members describe ONE identical mechanism (rare-edge power-schedule havoc amplification on a Host-header/cookie path); nothing to discriminate between them. This is a legitimately non-discriminable scheduling family; honest inconclusive is the correct outcome.
